prompt: Explain how the default axios instance is created and how an HTTP request flows through interceptors, dispatchRequest, and the transport adapter. Cite the source files that support the path.

expected_symbols.name: createInstance
expected_symbols.why: Factory that binds Axios methods onto the callable instance.

expected_symbols.name: Axios
expected_symbols.why: Owns defaults and request/response interceptor managers.

expected_symbols.name: Axios.prototype.request
expected_symbols.why: Merges config and runs the interceptor chain ending in dispatchRequest.

expected_symbols.name: dispatchRequest
expected_symbols.why: Transforms data, selects an adapter, and settles the response.

expected_symbols.name: InterceptorManager
expected_symbols.why: Registers fulfilled/rejected handlers for request and response chains.

expected_claims.text: createInstance wraps an Axios context and exposes verb helpers bound to request.
Axios.request merges defaults, runs request interceptors, then calls dispatchRequest.
dispatchRequest transforms the body/headers and invokes the configured adapter.
InterceptorManager stores interceptor pairs used by the promise chain in request.
adapters.js selects xhr or http transport based on environment capabilities.

forbidden_claims.text: axios sends HTTP requests directly from createInstance without an Axios request chain.
Interceptors run only after the transport adapter returns a response.

notes: Generalization holdout for retrieval retrieval. Do not tune planner or ranker heuristics against this manifest.
